item.title: Intra-party democracy key to women’s political participation: discussion
item.description: Speakers at a workshop on Saturday stressed the need for stronger internal democracy within political parties to ensure the meaningful participation of women in politics....

item.title: Scholarly journalist, DU teacher Shahed Kamal dies
item.description: Veteran journalist, former news editor of national news agency BSS and adjunct faculty of mass communication and journalism department of Dhaka University Shahed Kamal died of old-age complications at a city hospital on Saturday....

item.title: UGC clarifies research budget for public univs, allocates Tk 226cr
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh in a statement on Saturday issued a clarification regarding the recent incomplete media reports about the research budget allocation for public universities in the 2026–2027 financial year....

item.title: HC asks police to investigate attacks on bauls, fakirs, ascetics
item.description: The High Court has asked the police to investigate repeated attacks on bauls, fakirs, ascetics, shrines and khankahs and ensure the protection of their constitutional rights....

item.title: DSCC to fix sitting places for hawkers in city: administrator
item.description: Dhaka South City Corporation is going to introduce sitting plans and identity cards for hawkers in its area to regulate their professions and additionally the corporation would introduce holiday markets to help manage the city’s street vending....

item.title: Banks sit on Tk 3.28 lakh crore excess liquidity
item.description: Excess liquidity in Bangladesh’s banking sector remained above Tk 3 lakh crore in May 2026, as banks continue to hold back from lending amid a soaring defaults, sluggish investment demand and persistent economic uncertainty......

item.title: 139 more hospitalised with dengue in 24 hours
item.description: One hundred and thirty-nine people were admitted to hospitals across the country with dengue in the past 24 hours ending on Saturday....

item.title: Tax cuts fail to lower commodities prices
item.description: Consumers have yet to see any benefit from the tax reductions on around 60 essential commodities announced in the national budget for FY2026-27, as prices of most daily necessities have remained unchanged and some have increased even after the budget was passed on June 30......

item.title: Waste-to-energy project raises pollution, public health concerns
item.description: Environmental activists on Saturday expressed concern that the proposed waste-to-energy project at the Amin Bazar landfill under the Dhaka North City Corporation would increase air pollution and pose serious threat to public health....

item.title: US investors asked to expand FDI, assured of level playing field
item.description: The government on Friday urged US companies to increase investment in Bangladesh, pledging policy stability, a level playing field and a business-friendly environment as it seeks to boost foreign direct investment and deepen bilateral economic ties......

item.title: Govt to import another 500,000 tonnes of fuel to boost reserves
item.description: The government is procuring another 500,000 tonnes of fuel oil to strengthen the country’s fuel reserves amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, aiming to ensure uninterrupted economic and commercial activities and meet domestic energy demand......

item.title: Bangladesh courier sector grows steadily
item.description: Bangladesh’s courier service sector has steadily grown in employment, turnover, and productivity over the past two fiscal years, though it remains predominantly male-led and largely comprised of individually owned businesses, according to a Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics report released on Thursday......

item.title: US embassy marks 250th Independence Day in Dhaka
item.description: The United States embassy in Dhaka on Saturday marked the 250th anniversary of American independence with a musical programme at the South Plaza of Jatiya Sangsad Bhaban, highlighting the long-standing partnership between Bangladesh and the United States....

item.title: Ensuring fair price for farmers a priority for govt: minister
item.description: Agriculture, and also fisheries and livestock minister Mohammad Amin Ur Rashid on Saturday said ensuring fair prices of agricultural products produced by farmers is one of the government’s top priorities......

item.title: Bangladesh moving towards trillion-dollar economy: Khosru
item.description: Finance minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Saturday said Bangladesh is moving towards becoming a trillion-dollar economy, driven by growing domestic and foreign investments and the government’s long-term development strategy......

item.title: Dragon fruit trade booms at Jhenaidah wholesale market
item.description: GAURINATHPUR Bazar in Jhenaidah’s Maheshpur upazila has emerged as one of the country’s largest wholesale trading hubs for dragon fruit, with transactions worth several crores of taka taking place every day during the peak harvesting season......

item.title: NCP to launch nationwide July marches on Monday
item.description: The National Citizen Party on Saturday announced a nationwide programme titled ‘July March 2026’ to press for the implementation of the 2026 referendum, creation of employment opportunities and stronger border security....

item.title: Bumper summer brinjal harvest brings hope to Dinajpur farmers
item.description: In the fertile lands of Khansama upazila of the district, rows of lush green brinjal fields are yielding an abundant harvest this summer, offering a welcome boost to farmers who rely heavily on seasonal crops......

item.title: GI product betel leaf’s price down due to excess supply
item.description: Rajshahi’s betel leaf has long held a distinct reputation in the country’s market for its taste, aroma, and quality......

item.title: River erosion victims want sustainable livelihood
item.description: Reshma Begum, a 23-year-old mother of two toddlers and a victim of Jamuna River erosion, struggles to manage family expenses with her rickshaw driver husband’s meagre income....

item.title: AI to be used to stabilize economy, market: Muktadir
item.description: Commerce, industries, textiles and jute minister Khandakar Abdul Muktadir on Friday said the government will introduce Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology to monitor the country’s market situation and help maintain price stability......

item.title: Pope urges Europe to protect migrants
item.description: Pope Leo XIV on Saturday pressed Europe to do more to protect and integrate migrants as he visited Italy’s Lampedusa island, a major port of call for those risking the perilous crossing from Africa....

item.title: India approves buying $5.46b defence equipment
item.description: India approved on Friday the purchase of a range of military equipment for its defence forces worth $5.46 billion, including missiles, electronic warfare systems and Kamikaze drones......

item.title: Mild heatwave grips 14 dists, Rangpur div
item.description: A mild heatwave has engulfed large swathes of Bangladesh, covering 14 districts and the entire division of Rangpur, according to the Bangladesh Meteorological Department. ...

item.title: Terminal relocation demands more than policy decision
item.description: THE intercity bus terminals in Dhaka have for long been regarded as a major contributor to the traffic congestion. Traffic congestion is estimated to cost Dhaka’s economy about Tk 37,000 crore annually while wasting around five million work hours each year. Rough estimates suggest that around 14,000 city buses and 8,000 inter-district buses operate in Dhaka, yet the city......City& Local Guides

item.title: CID must be held to account for reserve theft probe failure
item.description: THE additional chief metropolitan magistrate’s court on July 2 posted August 9 for the Criminal Investigation Department to submit the investigation report in the case filed over the theft of $101 million from the reserve of the Bangladesh Bank kept at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. This followed the Criminal Investigation Department’s failure to submit the report. What......

item.title: Why it happened, what to do
item.description: THE July 2024 mass uprising did not just bring about a change in power in politics. It also brought together many long-standing political realities. The character of the state, political culture, citizen’s participation and ideological competition have all become topics of discussions anew as a result of this unprecedented student-led mass uprising. One of the most......

item.title: Next step in labour migration
item.description: BANGLADESH has emerged as one of the world’s leading labour-sending countries, with millions of migrant workers contributing to economic development both at home and abroad. Their remittances remain a cornerstone of the economy, strengthening foreign exchange reserves, supporting millions of households and helping reduce poverty......

item.title: The ‘land of the free’
item.description: EVERY year, the United States celebrates its founding principles of liberty, democracy, and the belief that ‘all men are created equal’. While these ideals transformed political thought and inspired global democratic movements, they coexist with a more troubling history tied to the nation’s achievements. The rise of the United States as a continental power involved not just......

item.title: Europe’s moral bankruptcy
item.description: EUROPEAN Commission president Ursula von der Leyen has every right to condition European relations with any other country or bloc on respect for human rights. That, of course, would hold true if she genuinely cared about such values herself......

item.title: Norway must handle occasion against Brazil: Solbakken
item.description: Norway coach Stale Solbakken urged his players not to be overawed by the occasion when they face five-time World Cup winners Brazil in the last 16 on Sunday....

item.title: ICT minister inaugurates development projects at Madhupur
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology and science and technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Saturday inaugurated and laid the foundation stones of several important development projects in the public health and education sectors at Madhupur in Tangail....

item.title: SEU holds discussion on measles prevention, awareness
item.description: The pharmacy department of Southeast University in collaboration with the Southeast Pharmacy Club organised a panel discussion titled Measles Awareness and Prevention: Critical Review from National Health Experts at the university in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: CMU ends 2-day training on digital filing system
item.description: A two-day skill development training on the use and implementation of digital filing system concluded at Chittagong Medical University on Saturday....

item.title: Ruthless Morocco break Canadian hearts to reach quarters
item.description: Canada became the first of the co-hosts to exit the World Cup as a clinical Morocco won 3-0 to reach the quarter-finals on Saturday in Houston....

item.title: BMU holds capping ceremony, orientation for new students
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University held the capping ceremony for the 15th batch of BSc in nursing and the orientation for the 4th batch of MSc in nursing at the university in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: Ensuring fair prices for farmers’ produce govt priority: minister
item.description: Fisheries, livestock and agriculture minister Mohammed Aminur Rashid has said that ensuring fair prices for farmers’ produce remains a top priority for the government....

item.title: 15 Jamaat activists injured in alleged BNP attack at Gulistan
item.description: At least 15 activists of Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami were injured as activists of Bangladesh Nationalist Party allegedly attacked them before a procession in the capital’s Gulistan area on Saturday....

item.title: Rising river water triggers massive erosion at 33 points in Kurigram
item.description: Flood fears are mounting in the northern border district of Kurigram as the water levels of major rivers continue to rise following days of heavy rainfall and upstream hill runoff....

item.title: Govt targets nationwide railway coverage: state minister
item.description: State minister for road transport and highways division Habibur Rashid on Saturday said that the government is working with the goal of gradually bringing all districts under the railway network....

item.title: Govt issues 6 directives for hospital cleanliness
item.description: The government on Saturday issued six special directives for all hospitals aimed at ensuring round-the-clock cleanliness marking completion of its 180 days in office....

item.title: Public awareness key to prevent social problems: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister and BNP Secretary General Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Saturday said various social problems can be effectively prevented by increasing public awareness....

item.title: None to be forgiven for corruption: minister Zahid
item.description: Social welfare minister Abu Zafar Md Zahid Hossain has said that no one would be forgiven for dishonesty as the present government has adopted a zero tolerance policy against corruption....

item.title: Govt pushes tech-driven modern Bangladesh: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Saturday said the present government is gradually driving the country towards a technology-driven modern Bangladesh....

item.title: University of Chankharpul hits cinemas in Cumilla, Rajshahi
item.description: The directorial venture of filmmaker Akash Haque titled The University of Chankharpul has expanded its theatrical run with releases in Cumilla and Rajshahi....
